items.initial_reaction_rate_unit: U/mg (for reduction of benzaldehyde at pH 7.5, 25°C)
items.special_treatment: Initial rate activities were measured spectrophotometrically at 340 nm by following the consumption of NADPH at 30°C in semi-micro cuvettes (total volume 1 mL) for 1 min. One unit (U) of enzyme is defined as the amount of enzyme that catalyzes the consumption of 1 μmol of NADPH per min at 30°C for the respective substrate. Standard reduction assay: 10 mmol/L benzaldehyde, 0.2 mmol/L NADPH, 50 mmol/L TEA–HCl buffer pH 7.5, 0.8 mmol/L CaCl2. Specific activities and kinetic parameters for various substrates are provided in Tables II–V.

items.initial_reaction_rate_unit: U/mg (Vmax for cyclohexanone reduction, Table V)
items.special_treatment: Kinetic parameters for cyclohexanone reduction: Vmax = 18.8 ± 0.6 U/mg, Km = 10.8 ± 1.2 mmol/L, measured in TEA–HCl buffer (50 mmol/L + 0.8 mmol/L CaCl2), pH 7.5, at 30°C, with 0.2 mmol/L NADPH.

Kinetic parameters for (R)-2-HPP: Vmax = 486.8 ± 53.2 U/mg, Km = 1.6 ± 0.4 mmol/L, Ki = 16.4 mmol/L (substrate inhibition), measured in TEA–HCl buffer (50 mmol/L + 0.8 mmol/L CaCl2), pH 7.5, at 30°C, with 0.2 mmol/L NADPH.

items.initial_reaction_rate_unit: U/mg (Vmax for cyclohexanol oxidation, Table V)
items.special_treatment: Kinetic parameters for cyclohexanol oxidation: Vmax = 7.7 ± 0.2 U/mg, Km = 5.2 ± 0.6 mmol/L, measured in TRIS–HCl buffer (50 mmol/L + 0.8 mmol/L CaCl2), pH 9.0, at 30°C, with 0.8 mmol/L NADP+.

items.initial_reaction_rate_unit: U/mg (Vmax for benzaldehyde reduction, Table V)
items.special_treatment: Kinetic parameters for benzaldehyde reduction: Vmax = 12.6 ± 0.3 U/mg, Km = 15.6 ± 0.9 mmol/L, measured in TEA–HCl buffer (50 mmol/L + 0.8 mmol/L CaCl2), pH 7.5, at 30°C, with 0.2 mmol/L NADPH.
Highest specific activity for aldehyde reduction was observed for 2-bromobenzaldehyde (5 mmol/L substrate).
Very low activity for ethanol oxidation, only detectable at high substrate concentration (1.7 mol/L).
